text.title: Cache Invalidation (TTL, Event-Based, Tag-Based, Version-Based) Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules every Cache Invalidation (TTL, Event-Based, Tag-Based, Version-Based) application MUST enforce. HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Every cache key MUST have a TTL — even when other strategies apply.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Caching field manuals. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: When staleness SLO < TTL, an event-based purge MUST exist on the relevant write path.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Cache consistency reviews. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Tag-based invalidation MUST use Redis SETs of keys (`SADD tag:user:42 cache:user:42:profile`).

rationale.source: Redis docs + tag-based caching pattern catalogue

rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Redis docs + tag-based caching pattern catalogue. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Schema or computation changes MUST bump a version prefix (`v2:user:42`) instead of relying on TTL.

rationale.source: Stripe / GitHub eng blogs on schema migration

rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Stripe / GitHub eng blogs on schema migration. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Purge operations MUST be idempotent — retries on flaky bus MUST NOT cause wrong invalidations.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Distributed-systems best-practice. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: A `cache_staleness_seconds` SLO MUST be emitted per entity; alert when over budget.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: SRE workbook. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Writes MUST NOT populate caches — that is the cache-write-patterns concern, not invalidation.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Layering reviews. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

section.title: Conclusion targets used by the decision tree

rule.statement: Run the full procedure; emit an artefact passing the output contract.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions hold and no duplicate exists.

rule.statement: Skip; surface the missing precondition; do not synthesize.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions fail.

rule.statement: Update the existing artefact in place (bump version, refresh last_reviewed).
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when a duplicate is found.
